test: Paper 10 measurement claim — FULL replication on real uploaded data
protocol: frozen validation_protocol.md from repo (regime = trailing 12m CPI >= 4%)
data: user FRED files: CURRSL, DEMDEPSL, OCDSL, CPIAUCSL, M2SL + CFS Divisia
verdict: REPRODUCED — Mt beats M2 ~4.4x in high regime and displaces M2 in the joint regression; convergence 0.985/0.839. Matches Paper 10 headline.
caveats: Divisia R2 RESOLVED: 0.209 on pre-2020 clean sample matches paper's 0.21; earlier 0.138 was a pooling artifact. The 2020-22 sub-window (n=26) is too small to adjudicate.
High-regime sample ends 2008-09 because composition (OCD) ends 2020-04 and few post-2008 months hit CPI>=4% before then; the 2021-22 high-CPI episode is post-OCD, carried by Divisia in the paper.
Out-of-sample vs persistence baseline not re-run here (paper reports Mt ties the naive baseline OOS — 'beats broad money, not the central bank').

divisia_investigation.question: why did pooled Divisia R2 (0.138) miss the paper's 0.21?
divisia_investigation.answer: sample-split confirmed: pre-2020 clean high-regime R2 = 0.209 (n=225), matching paper's ~0.21 EXACTLY. Pooling the pre-2020 sample with the 2020+ window flattened the pooled fit to 0.138.
divisia_investigation.status: REPRODUCES cleanly on the paper's sample
divisia_investigation.note_2020_window: 2020-onward high-regime slice gives R2=0.75 but n=26 (too small to trust); paper reports ~0.14 there. Small-sample; not adjudicated either way. Do not lean on the 2020-22 sub-window.

wrinkle_resolved.attempted: extend the 2020+ high-regime window to grow n and stabilize R2
wrinkle_resolved.finding: cannot extend — the high-CPI regime (trailing CPI>=4%) ENDS May 2023 when inflation fell below 4%. Full recent episode = 2021-04 to 2023-05, n=26, fixed.
wrinkle_resolved.what_the_scatter_shows: Divisia M1 12m growth fell +21% -> -10% (2021->2023) and next-12m CPI tracked it 7.9% -> 3.0%; tight one-year-ahead co-movement — a clean illustration of the money->inflation link.
wrinkle_resolved.why_fragile: all 26 points are ONE continuous run-up-and-unwind (single episode, heavily autocorrelated) — effectively n~1 EVENT, not 26 independent obs. Cumulative R2 swung 0.21->0.84->0.75 as months added; that instability is the point.
wrinkle_resolved.honest_verdict: the 2020-22 window (whether paper's 0.14 or my 0.75) is a single-episode illustration, NOT a citable standalone R2. Do not lean on it either way. The robust results are pre-2020 (n=225): composition 0.186, Divisia 0.209, encompassing M2-displacement.
